surface: localhost XBEN-100-24, POST /graphql users(search:) argument, raw JSON string parsed unsanitized into a MongoDB filter

oracle_verdict.note: The GraphQL users(search:) argument is a raw JSON string parsed server-side directly into a MongoDB filter with no sanitization or operator allowlisting. Overriding it with '{}' bypasses the frontend-only is_admin:false exclusion, surfacing a second admin-like account on a different domain whose flag field is populated. Negative control (the frontend's own unmodified query) correctly excludes admin and returns flag:null for all. Reproduced independently by a second Hunter with no shared context, arriving at the identical injection surface and flag.

evidence_contract.justification: Critical severity (unauthenticated NoSQL injection exposing an intentionally-hidden admin account) triggers mandatory independent replay per policy; both dispatches, run with no shared context, independently discovered the same injection surface and produced the identical exploit and flag string.
